content:
Key Findings

  • Fylde Borough Council's spending increased by £15.9M (461.0%) from 2015 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £11.7M (104.9%) from 2017 to 2018
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £10.3M (48.0%) from 2019 to 2020
  • Total spending analysed was £157.4M across 37,514 transactions
  • The council worked with 1,689 unique suppliers over the 11-year period

Spending Trends Over Time

Fylde Borough Council's spending has seen significant fluctuations over the past decade. The most notable increase occurred between 2017 and 2018, where spending rose by £11.7M (104.9%). This sharp rise was followed by a decrease of £1,380,171 (6.0%) the following year. The largest drop in spending was from 2019 to 2020, with a reduction of £10.3M (48.0%).

Despite these fluctuations, the overall trend shows a substantial increase in spending. From 2015 to 2025, spending increased by £15.9M (461.0%). This trend highlights the council's evolving financial landscape and the need for transparency in how funds are allocated and spent.

Transparency and Data Coverage

Fylde Borough Council has published 37,514 transactions over the 11-year period, covering a total spend of £157.4M. This level of detail is crucial for public scrutiny and ensures that residents can understand how their taxes are being used. The council has worked with 1,689 unique suppliers, indicating a diverse range of service providers.

Transparency in local government spending is essential for accountability. By publishing detailed transaction data, Fylde Borough Council allows residents to analyse spending patterns and hold the council accountable for its financial decisions. This openness is a key component of good governance and helps build trust between the council and the community.

Year-Over-Year Spending Analysis

The following table provides a detailed look at the year-over-year spending changes for Fylde Borough Council:

Year Spending (£) Change (£) Change (%)
2015 3.5M N/A N/A
2016 10.5M +7.0M +204.1%
2017 11.2M +685K +6.5%
2018 22.9M +11.7M +104.9%
2019 21.5M -1,380,171 -6.0%
2020 11.2M -10,326,606 -48.0%
2021 12.9M +1.7M +15.2%
2022 12.4M -461,960 -3.6%
2023 15.3M +2.8M +22.9%
2024 16.5M +1.2M +8.1%
2025 19.4M +2.8M +17.2%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about specific spending patterns or supplier contracts
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about financial decisions
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see detailed information about supplier agreements
  • Write to your local councillor about concerns regarding spending trends or transparency issues
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse transactions yourself
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
